---
title: "Actuarial GLM Model Report"
format: html
---

# Executive Summary

Describe model purpose, target, selected model, key variables, validation performance, major judgments, and limitations.

# Data Reconciliation and Methodology

Document data source, extract date, grain, experience period, exposure basis, claim/loss definition, premium basis where relevant, exclusions, transformations, and reconciliation of exposure, claim count, loss, and premium.

# Target and Family Selection

Document user direction mode, locked expert choices, inferred choices, target unit, one-part versus two-part deliverable, candidate families considered, rejected families, selected family/link, offset or weights, diagnostics used, and selection rationale.

# Split Rationale

Document train/validation/test split method, period boundaries or random seed, and why the split is appropriate for pricing.

# Model Specification

Document family/link, offset or weights, selected predictors, candidate models considered, rejected candidates, and rationale for the selected model.

# Diagnostics

Include performance table, lift, calibration, actual versus expected, actual versus predicted aggregate loss where applicable, actual versus predicted pure premium where applicable, finite RMSE/MAE or equivalent error metrics, residual review, overdispersion for count models, exploding prediction checks, and segment stability.

# Relativities

Include raw modeled relativities, selected relativities, base levels or normalization, credibility adjustments, and manual overrides.

# Regulatory and Fairness Review

Document variable eligibility assumptions, jurisdictional caveats, proxy concerns, and recommended review.

# Limitations and Monitoring

Document data limitations, operational scoring concerns, monitoring metrics, and refresh recommendations.

# Internal Run Notes

Document skill usability notes, implementation caveats, package/setup issues, and follow-up work. Omit this section from client-facing actuarial reports unless requested.
